skill.name: axiom-audit
skill.description: Audit Axiom logs to identify and prioritize errors and warnings, research probable causes, and flag log smells. Use when user asks to check Axiom logs, analyze production errors, investigate log issues, or audit logging patterns.
skill.summary: Audit Axiom logs, prioritize errors, and produce evidence-based incident reports.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis over-reported markdown code fences as Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography. The confirmed risks are legitimate but meaningful: the skill instructs agents to install and run external CLIs, use Axiom credentials, query a remote Axiom service, inspect environment files, and write audit reports.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Installation And Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to install axiom-mcp and mcptools, then run mcp commands. This is legitimate for an Axiom audit workflow, but it executes third-party binaries and should be reviewed by the user before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit setup and usage instructions, not hidden execution. Risk remains because they run external software and make operational queries.

medium_findings.title: Axiom Credential And Environment File Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill asks users to set AXIOM_TOKEN and search repository environment files for Axiom settings. This can expose secrets to the agent context if users run it in repositories with real credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly references AXIOM_TOKEN and grep over environment-like files. This is a clear credential-handling concern, even if the intent is operational setup.

medium_findings.title: Remote Log Query Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to query Axiom datasets for errors and warnings. This is expected behavior, but it sends authenticated requests to an external observability service and may retrieve sensitive production log data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow is clearly designed for Axiom queries. The security concern depends on the user's log contents and permissions, so risk is medium rather than high.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are markdown code fences and inline report examples, not executable Ruby backticks. They still document shell commands, which is covered by the medium external command finding.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding file is markdown skill documentation, and the detected characters delimit fenced examples. No Ruby code or dynamic backtick execution is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain descriptive text and APL sorting examples, not cryptographic functions or weak hash algorithms. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not contain crypto APIs, algorithms, or security-sensitive hashing. The static labels do not match the observed file context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands In Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes installation and runtime shell commands for Go, Homebrew, grep, and mcp. Users should approve these commands before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are visible and part of the intended workflow. They are not obfuscated, but they affect local tooling and credentials.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Report File Write
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to create an audit report under .audits. This is normal output generation, but it requires filesystem write access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write target is explicit and limited to an audit report path. Risk is reduced because the skill also says audit only and no fixes.

content.user_title: Audit Axiom Logs for Production Issues
content.value_statement: Production logs can hide repeated errors, noisy warnings, and missing context. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Axiom queries, prioritization, and evidence-based reporting.
actual_capabilities: Installs and uses axiom-mcp and mcptools for Axiom access.
Lists Axiom datasets before selecting a log source.
Runs APL queries for errors, warnings, and seven-day trends.
Prioritizes issues using severity and frequency scoring.
Researches source code context for probable causes.
Creates a structured markdown audit report under .audits.
limitations: Requires valid Axiom credentials and access to the target datasets.
Depends on consistent log fields such as level, message, and error_type.
Does not fix code or modify production systems.
May expose sensitive log or environment data in the agent context.

use_cases.title: Production Error Triage
use_cases.description: Identify high-frequency errors and warnings from recent Axiom logs before an incident review.

use_cases.title: Release Health Review
use_cases.description: Compare recent error trends after a deployment and find new or increasing failure patterns.

use_cases.title: Logging Quality Audit
use_cases.description: Find missing context, noisy messages, sensitive data exposure, and other log quality problems.

prompt_templates.title: Run A Basic Log Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check of recent errors and warnings
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit our Axiom logs for the last 24 hours. List datasets, choose the most relevant logs dataset, and summarize top errors and warnings.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Production Errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Query Axiom for production errors from the last 24 hours. Prioritize them using P0 to P4 scoring and explain the evidence for each priority.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate Error Sources
prompt_templates.scenario: Connect log patterns to code context
prompt_templates.prompt: For each recurring Axiom error, search the codebase for likely sources, read the surrounding code, and identify probable causes with supporting evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare An Incident Audit Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a full Axiom audit report with trend analysis, error categories, log smells, source references, and an executive summary for incident review.

output_examples.input: Audit the last 24 hours of production logs.
output_examples.output: A prioritized audit summary with total errors, total warnings, top P0 and P1 issues, probable causes, and a link to the report.

output_examples.input: Find logging problems in our Axiom data.
output_examples.output: A log quality review that highlights excessive logging, missing request context, vague messages, and possible sensitive data exposure.

output_examples.input: Review errors after the latest deployment.
output_examples.output: A trend-focused report that separates new, increasing, resolved, and unchanged errors with source evidence where available.

best_practices: Use read-only Axiom credentials with the smallest required dataset scope.
Review planned shell commands before allowing installation or MCP execution.
Redact secrets, personal data, and customer identifiers before sharing reports.
anti_patterns: Running the skill with broad production credentials in an untrusted workspace.
Treating frequency alone as severity without checking user impact.
Publishing raw log samples that contain tokens, emails, or private identifiers.

faq.question: Does this skill require Axiom access?
faq.answer: Yes. It needs valid Axiom credentials and permission to read the target datasets.

faq.question: Can it fix the errors it finds?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for audit and reporting only.

faq.question: Why does it need Bash access?
faq.answer: It uses Bash to install tools, run MCP commands, search files, and create the audit report.

faq.question: Does it send data outside my environment?
faq.answer: It queries Axiom through axiom-mcp. Review credentials and dataset scope before running it.

faq.question: What report does it create?
faq.answer: It creates a markdown audit report with priorities, trends, probable causes, evidence, and log smells.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive logs?
faq.answer: Use caution. Logs may contain secrets or personal data, so restrict access and redact report output.
